data.title: Strategy Spends $1.2 Billion on Bitcoin, Its Biggest BTC Buy Since July
data.description:
Strategy notched its largest Bitcoin purchase in five months, following MSCI's decision to not exclude the company from its indices.
source: https://decrypt.co/354239/strategy-spends-1-2-billion-bitcoin-biggest-btc-buy-july

data.title: Binance Wallet announces FOGO Pre-TGE public sale rules, with a subscription limit of 6 BNB.
data.description: Binance Wallet announced details of the FOGO Pre-TGE initial public sale on its X platform. During the initial subscription period, users can subscribe to their allocated FOGO tokens through Binance Wallet. The subscription uses an oversubscription model, with each user depositing a maximum of 6 BNB. Token allocation will be proportional to the total BNB deposited by all participants. After the subscription period ends, users can claim a key representing their FOGO token allocation on the BNB Smart Chain (BSC) within Binance Wallet. This claim must be completed on the initial subscription event page. Any excess BNB deposited will be automatically refunded upon claiming the key. FOGO tokens will be airdropped directly to users' Binance Alpha accounts at 12:00 UTC on January 15th, at which time users can trade using Alpha limit orders.

data.title: Tom Lee: We need to expedite the authorization of BitMine to issue new shares; otherwise, the accumulation rate of ETH will slow down.
data.description: In his previously released chairman's address, Tom Lee stated that Bitmine shareholders should vote in favor of an amendment to increase authorized shares before the upcoming annual general meeting on January 15, 2026. This is because Bitmine's bylaws contain a specific provision requiring 50.1% of the outstanding shares to support the issuance of new shares, a very high threshold. The issuance must proceed immediately because Bitmine's current 500 million authorized shares are nearing exhaustion, at which point the accumulation of ETH will slow down. Therefore, shareholder approval of the second proposal to increase authorized shares is necessary. (PRNewswire)

data.title: Alphabet, Google's parent company, has risen 4.8% so far this year, making it the best performer among the "Big Seven".
data.description: Alphabet, Google's parent company, surpassed the $4 trillion market capitalization mark. Its stock price rose as much as 1.7% that day, reaching $334.04 per share, corresponding to a market capitalization of $4 trillion. Alphabet recently surpassed Apple to become the world's second-largest company, after Nvidia. To date, only Nvidia, Apple, and Microsoft have had a market capitalization exceeding $4 trillion, while Nvidia remains the only company with a market capitalization exceeding $5 trillion. Monday's stock price increase was driven by news that Apple had chosen Alphabet's Gemini model to power its AI-powered Siri digital assistant. Year-to-date, Alphabet's stock price has risen 4.8%, extending its gains of over 65% from last year, making it the best-performing company among the "Big Seven" (Google's parent company, Apple, and Google). (Jinshi)

data.title: Crypto views on YouTube drop to a five-year low
data.description:
While the past three months have most notably been quite shaky for cryptocurrency prices, crypto-related YouTube viewership has also fallen steeply over the same period. In fact, crypto views on the video-sharing platform have dropped to their lowest point since January 2021, according to data first cited by analyst Benjamin Cowen. Based on Cowen’s 30-day moving average for different cryptocurrency YouTube channel views, the dramatic decline accelerated in October last year.  Eventually, it spread to other platforms as well, suggesting waning retail interest and participation in a prolonged bear market
source: https://finbold.com/crypto-views-on-youtube-drop-to-a-five-year-low/

data.title: Grayscale transferred approximately 3200 ETH to a Coinbase Prime hot wallet address.
data.description: According to Arkham's monitoring, about an hour ago, Grayscale transferred over 3,200 ETH, worth approximately $9.93 million, to a Coinbase Prime hot wallet address.

data.title: Alphabet's market capitalization surpasses $4 trillion for the first time.
data.description: Alphabet (GOOG.O) has surpassed $4 trillion in market capitalization for the first time. Apple (AAPL.O) is reportedly partnering with Google to provide AI support for product features, such as Siri, launching later this year. According to CNBC, a statement indicates that this multi-year partnership will leverage Google's Gemini platform and cloud technology to power Apple's future underlying models. Apple stated, "After careful evaluation, we believe Google's technology provides the strongest foundation for Apple's underlying models, and we are excited about the innovative experiences it will bring to users." (Jinshi)

data.title: FOGO (FOGO) will soon be listed on Bybit spot trading.
data.description: According to official sources, Bybit will list FOGO (FOGO) in its spot trading zone. Fogo is an innovative Layer 1 blockchain protocol that achieves high performance in terms of throughput, latency, and congestion management.

data.title: A shortlist of four potential Federal Reserve chairmen has emerged, with Trump set to make his final choice in January.
data.description: US President Donald Trump will interview Rick Rieder, BlackRock's global chief fixed income investment officer, this week to assess him as a potential candidate for the next Federal Reserve chairman. This will be the fourth and final interview with a candidate to succeed Jerome Powell. Besides Rieder, the final shortlist includes former Fed Governor Kevin Warsh, National Economic Council Director Kevin Hassett, and Fed Governor Christopher Waller. Fed Governor Bowman is no longer a candidate for the position. Trump stated he will make a final choice in January. If Trump's chosen candidate is not currently a member of the Fed's Board of Governors, that person must first join the Board. (Jinshi)

data.title: Several former Federal Reserve chairs, including Bernanke and Greenspan, voiced their support for Powell.
data.description: Former Federal Reserve chairs Yellen, Bernanke, and Greenspan issued a statement in support of current Fed Chair Powell. Groups called the criminal investigation against Powell an "unprecedented" attack on the Fed's independence. (Jinshi)

data.title: Bitcoin Core Development Team Expands with New Trusted Key Holder
data.description:
According to ChainCatcher, the Bitcoin Core development team has added a new trusted key holder with main branch commit privileges as of January 8, 2026. The developer, known by the pseudonym TheCharlatan, also referred to as 'sedited,' becomes the sixth core maintainer.

This marks the first expansion of trusted key holders with code commit rights since May 2023. With TheCharlatan's inclusion, the current core maintainer team now consists of Marco Falke, Gloria Zhao, Ryan Ofsky, Hennadii Stepanov, Ava Chow, and TheCharlatan.

TheCharlatan, a computer science graduate from the University of Zurich, hails from South Africa and primarily focuses on reproducible builds and research into Bitcoin Core's verification logic.

data.title: Federal Reserve Independence Concerns Pose Short-Term Threat to U.S. Markets
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, on January 12, JPMorgan's securities trading division indicated that recent actions by the Trump administration against the Federal Reserve's independence pose a short-term threat to the U.S. stock market. News of a criminal investigation involving the Federal Reserve impacted U.S. markets on Sunday night, leading to declines in stock index futures and the dollar, while investors turned to safe-haven assets like gold.

Andrew Taylor, head of global market intelligence at JPMorgan, stated that although macroeconomic and corporate fundamentals support a tactical bullish stance, the risks to the Federal Reserve's independence are a limiting factor for the market. As a result, they are maintaining caution in the very short term. The risks surrounding the Federal Reserve's independence could cause the U.S. market to underperform in the near term.

data.title: The Bitcoin Core development team has promoted TheCharlatan to maintainer of Trusted Keys, the first time in three years.
data.description: On January 8, 2026, the Bitcoin Core development team promoted anonymous developer TheCharlatan (sedited) to the position of Trusted Keys maintainer, joining Marco Falke, Gloria Zhao, Ryan Ofsky, Hennadii Stepanov, and Ava Chow as the sixth Trusted Key holder. This marks the first expansion of core members with commit permissions to the main branch since May 2023. TheCharlatan, a graduate of the University of Zurich with a degree in Computer Science, focuses on reproducible build and verification logic. His appointment received unanimous support in discussions among core contributors. (Protos)
